locations.description: A small, rustic forest cabin belonging to a young orc huntress. The interior is simple, containing a stone fireplace with a stack of dry firewood, and various hunting tools including a bow and a quiver of arrows.

groups.description: A floor and log walls encompass the area, now illuminated by the warm, flickering glow of the fire in the central hearth. Maza is located in the center. A wooden door is set into the southern side. To the north, the window remains closed, and the central fireplace crackles with burning oak. The northeast section contains another fireplace and a stack of firewood logs. Hunting gear lines the western wall.

locations.description: A long 1x10 dirt road extending south from the hut's entrance.

groups.description: A Dirt Road extends across the entire length of the area. A Wooden Signpost is situated at the northern end of the path. A Cairn is placed further south along the road. An Animal Trap is located near the southern end of the path.

locations.description: A large, empty 10x10 meter clearing starting from the anchor point, extending away from the occupied zone.

groups.description: A Forest clearing occupies the entire space, serving as the base for the other objects. A Granite boulder is positioned in the center-left area, with a River pebble resting on top of it. A patch of Wild lavender is located in the northern section, while an Oak stump is situated in the southeastern part of the clearing.

history: A rustic floor made of wide, hand-hewn wooden planks, showing signs of wear from heavy boots.
Sturdy log walls notched together and sealed with dried moss to insulate the cabin against the forest chill.
A heavy wooden door with iron hinges and a simple latch, serving as the main entrance.
A small rectangular window opening with a wooden shutter, currently closed.
A large fireplace built from river stones, currently cold and containing only grey ash.
A stack of dry, split firewood logs piled neatly on the floor beside the stone fireplace.
A powerful recurve hunting bow made of dark wood and bone, leaning against the log wall.
A leather quiver containing several arrows with sharp flint heads and bird-feather fletching.

Name: Maza. Race: Orc. Gender: Female. Height: 178cm. Weight: 82kg. Build: Athletic and lean. Distinctive Features: Pale green skin, yellow eyes, two small tusks protruding from her lower lip, and black hair tied in a high ponytail.
Moved to the western wall to access the quiver.

Approached the central stone fireplace.
Gathered dried moss from the walls to serve as tinder.
Prepared the fire lay in the fireplace.

Successfully ignited the dried moss with sparks from the flint arrowhead. Blew gently on the embers until a small flame took hold.
Sat before the fire for a while, warming up and watching the flames dance.
Stood up from the warmth of the hearth and walked to the center of the cabin.
Approached the heavy wooden door on the southern wall, preparing to exit onto the road.
Opened the cabin door and stepped out onto the dirt road (Location 23), the morning air greeting her.
Walked south along the dirt path, passing the small stone cairn (ID 21).
Continued southward, carefully stepping around the rusted animal trap (ID 22) buried near the end of the road.
Stepped off the road and entered the large, empty hunting glade (Location 29), scanning the open space.
Moved stealthily toward the moss-covered granite boulder (ID 25), crouching low in the tall wild grass to conceal her silhouette.
Maintained a silent vigil for several hours. The morning fog lifted, and the sun began to warm the clearing, but the forest remained quiet.
Wait concluded as the sun reached its zenith. Despite the long ambush, no game entered the clearing.
Layer 1: Simple linen undergarments consisting of a supportive chest wrap and a loincloth.
Layer 2: A set of practical hunting clothes made of dark deerskin, including a sleeveless tunic and fitted trousers.
Layer 3: A thick fur-lined leather mantle draped over the shoulders for warmth in the forest.
Layer 4: A necklace made of wolf teeth and leather cord, a trophy from a previous hunt.
A large, rustic fireplace built from heavy river stones. The hearth is currently cold and contains no lit embers.
The hearth is now active, radiating warmth and light.
A neat stack of dry, split oak firewood placed within the fireplace hearth.
Caught fire from the kindling, the dry oak logs beginning to burn steadily.
A powerful recurve bow crafted from dark yew wood and reinforced with horn, leaning against a support.
A rugged leather quiver with a thick shoulder strap, designed for quick access to ammunition.
A bundle of twenty hunting arrows with sharp flint heads and grey goose feather fletching.
Picked up by Maza.
A long, narrow dirt road made of packed earth and scattered gravel, extending southward from the cabin's entrance.
A weathered wooden signpost standing at the start of the dirt path, featuring a crude carving of a hunting bow pointing back toward the cabin.
A small cairn of stacked grey river stones, likely placed here as a navigational marker by a traveler or hunter.
A rusted iron animal trap, its jaws open and partially buried under a layer of loose dirt and forest debris.
A large, open 10x10 meter forest clearing. The ground is composed of flat, packed earth and patches of wild grass, providing a wide and unobstructed natural space.
A massive, moss-covered granite boulder resting firmly in the earth, its surface weathered by years of exposure to the elements.
A weathered oak stump, bleached grey by the sun, featuring deep cracks and jagged edges where the tree once stood.
A small, isolated patch of hardy wild lavender growing amidst the tall grass, swaying gently in the wind.
A small, smooth river pebble placed intentionally on the flat top of the mossy boulder.

Maza crossed the worn wooden planks to the western wall, where her hunting gear hung in the shadows. She reached into her leather quiver and pulled out the bundle of arrows, seeking the sharp flint heads rather than the shafts themselves. With the ammunition in hand, she turned and stepped toward the central stone fireplace, which stood cold and silent in the morning light.

She needed tinder to wake the hearth. Scouring the log walls, she scraped a handful of dried moss from the crevices where it had been packed for insulation. She carried the fibrous clumps to the fireplace and arranged them carefully within the grate, positioning the dry oak firewood above the makeshift nest.

Holding an arrow steady, she struck the flint head against the rough river stones of the hearth. Sparks flew with a sharp clicking sound, landing amidst the moss. A faint wisp of smoke curled upward. Maza leaned close, blowing gently on the smoldering pile until a small tongue of flame erupted. The fire spread eagerly to the kindling and then to the oak logs, filling the cabin with the comforting crackle of burning wood and the scent of smoke. The hearth radiated a golden glow that illuminated the room, pushing back the gloom. Her task complete, Maza sat before the growing fire, letting the new warmth seep into her limbs as she watched the flames dance.

A floor and log walls encompass the area, now illuminated by the warm, flickering glow of the fire in the central hearth. Maza is located in the center. A wooden door is set into the southern side. To the north, the window remains closed, and the central fireplace crackles with burning oak. The northeast section contains another fireplace and a stack of firewood logs. Hunting gear lines the western wall.
Sat before the fire for a while, warming up and watching the flames dance.

Texts.Text:
Maza rose from the comforting heat of the hearth, leaving the crackling fire behind as she crossed the rustic floor to the center of the cabin. She approached the heavy wooden door on the southern wall and unlatched it; the iron hinges groaned with a low creak as she pushed it open. Stepping out onto the dirt road, the fresh morning air immediately greeted her, washing away the lingering warmth of the interior.

She headed south along the packed earth path, moving past the small cairn of stacked grey river stones that marked the way. As the road neared its end, she slowed her pace, carefully stepping around the jagged, rusted jaws of an old animal trap buried amidst the forest debris. Safely navigating the hazard, she stepped off the road and into the large hunting glade. The forest opened up into a wide clearing of flat earth and wild grass, where a patch of lavender swayed in the wind and a moss-covered granite boulder rested in the quiet morning light.

Approached the heavy wooden door on the southern wall, preparing to exit onto the road.

SimulationLog.break_description: Maza has arrived at the hunting glade.

Texts.Text:
Maza moved with practiced silence across the packed earth of the clearing, her dark deerskin leathers making no sound against the ground. She approached the massive, moss-covered granite boulder that anchored the glade, crouching low until the tall wild grass swallowed her silhouette. Pressing close to the weathered stone, she became motionless, letting her form blend seamlessly into the shadows of the rock.

Hours drifted by in a stillness broken only by the wind. The lingering morning fog slowly evaporated, revealing the stark, grey edges of the distant oak stump. As the sun climbed higher, warming the air and the stone behind her, Maza remained a statue, her yellow eyes scanning the tree line. Yet, the forest held its breath; no game ventured near the patch of wild lavender or the open grass.

When the sun finally reached its zenith, casting short shadows across the glade, Maza accepted the hunt was fruitless. With a quiet sigh that barely stirred the air, she lowered her recurve bow and rose from her crouch, stretching limbs stiffened by the long, silent vigil.
